sanskrit: राजविद्या राजगुह्यं पवित्रमिदमुत्तमम् । प्रत्यक्षावगमं धर्म्यं सुसुखं कर्तुमव्ययम् ॥
hindiTranslation: यह सम्पूर्ण विद्याओंका और सम्पूर्ण गोपनीयोंका राजा है ।  यह अति पवित्र तथा अतिश्रेष्ठ है और इसका फल भी प्रत्यक्ष है ।  यह धर्ममय है, अविनाशी है और करनेमें बहुत सुगम है अर्थात् इसको प्राप्त करना बहुत सुगम है ॥
englishTranslation: This is the king of all knowledge, the king of all secrets. It is supremely pure, directly knowable, aligned with dharma, easy to practice, and imperishable.
essence: The highest truth is both profound and simple to live.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: This teaching is called the highest knowledge because it is pure, direct, righteous, easy to practice, and never lost.
simpleInsight: The deepest truth does not make life harder; it makes life clearer and lighter.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen and Krishna begins revealing a teaching greater than ordinary wisdom. He names this knowledge as the highest, most secret, pure, direct, and practical path that can actually be lived.
modernRelevance: You are standing at a hospital corridor, waiting for a hard diagnosis. The clearest truth is not always the most complicated one; sometimes it is the one you can actually live by.
translationLiteral: राजविद्या (king of knowledge) / राजगुह्यं (king of secrets) / पवित्रम् (pure) / इदम् (this) / उत्तमम् (supreme) / प्रत्यक्ष-अवगमम् (directly knowable) / धर्म्यम् (aligned with dharma) / सुसुखम् (very easy) / कर्तुम् (to do) / अव्ययम् (imperishable)
insight: It shifts the mind from treating spiritual understanding as abstract to seeing it as immediate and usable.

quoteVariants.text: The highest knowledge is pure, direct, and easy to live.
The deepest knowing is also the simplest to practice.
Pure truth is easy to live.
